programme.desc: Kimble finds an escaped prisoner who is dangerously ill, claiming to be innocent, and determined to gun down the man he blames for framing him

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard Kimble dates a waitress whose nephew's buddies plan to capture him with a mail-order rifle

programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Team Scorpion continues to work with Mark Collins to prevent an extinction event. A last-minute decision could have terrible consequences for Cabe.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy officer scheduled to testify in a forthcoming murder trial goes missing after receiving a threat at the safe house where he was staying, and the deputy district attorney asks the NCIS agents to locate him. One of their few leads is a dead turkey Tony and McGee find in the man's fridge, so they take it to Ducky for an autopsy. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate the murder of a marine during a cruise. They discover he came from a famous and wealthy family but had turned his back on a life of privilege to join the military, and after the death of his father had inherited a large part of his business. His decision to change his will may have pushed one of his relatives to kill him to keep the firm in their control. Crime drama, guest-starring Diane Neal (Law & Order: Special Victims Unit)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy recruitment officer is murdered at a high school and the agents discover he had recently begun a relationship with a married teacher whose wife held a grudge against him. They also find out a student's father was vehemently opposed to his joining the military, and receive help from an unexpected quarter - Ducky's esteemed predecessor, Dr Magnus. Crime drama, guest starring comedian Bob Newhart

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A former serviceman who had been having an affair is beaten to death in his own back garden, and the agents are forced to consider the possibility that his wife - a US Marine and martial arts expert - is the killer. Meanwhile, McGee becomes the subject of an embarrassing mix-up when an inflatable doll apparently purchased on his credit card is delivered. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents are forced to undergo psychological evaluations conducted by Dr Rachel Cranston (guest star Wendy Makkena) while investigating the death of a submarine's commanding officer. Suspicion falls on a journalist seen arguing with the man before his demise, and Tony discovers a link between the psychotherapist shadowing the agents and Ziva's predecessor Kate Todd. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Team Scorpion must work with an old nemesis, Mark Collins. Meanwhile, Walter and Paige awkwardly navigate their new relationship.

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Team Scorpion continues to work with Mark Collins to prevent an extinction event. A last-minute decision could have terrible consequences for Cabe.

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A Navy officer dies following a confrontation involving a dancer and a Sudanese emigre at a strip club. However, when investigations reveal he was a code-breaker involved in naval intelligence, Callen and Sam dig deeper and gradually unearth links to Safar, a Libyan-born terrorist known to recruit on behalf of al-Qaeda. Henri Lubatti guest stars

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A taxi driver recently released from prison is murdered just hours after sending an e-mail to an old alias that Callen used when he went undercover to investigate three marines who stole $5million. Resuming the persona, Callen goes in search of the gang, one of whom is killed in a drive-by shooting

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen, Sam and the team spring into action when fellow agent Dom activates a distress signal. They track down his car, which is riddled with bullet holes, and Eric intercepts a signal from his phone, tracing it to a house that contains nothing but some used medical supplies and a dead body. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: After a mid-series break, the drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects returns. Sam and Callen investigate when five men wearing US Marine uniforms are found dead in a warehouse and discover they were killed by a lethal toxin that could be used as a bioweapon. Initial inquiries lead them to a former military chemist who now has Alzheimer's, while Callen recognises one of the victims as someone who used to work as hired muscle for the well-connected owner of a strip joint. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when an Islamic terrorist group in Iraq steals a truck with $5million and track the money back to America. The agents learn the cash has been used to set up a bank robbery intended as a ruse to allow a bank employee to transfer $100million of the terrorists' money that has been frozen by the government in an offshore bank. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: Mission: Impossible
programme.desc: Part three of three. A traitor puts the operation in danger and leaves Jim Phelps with only one option - a trained falcon

programme.title: Bondi Rescue
programme.desc: The work of lifeguards in Sydney, Australia, as they help deal with jellyfish stings, investigate shark sightings and tend people with health problems. In this edition, the summer season becomes even busier thanks to a host of emergency calls that turn out to be false alarms

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine dies while saving the life of a foreign defence secretary, who was the target of an assassination attempt. Gibbs and the team are given the task of protecting the politician's daughter while she studies in America, but fail to stop her being kidnapped and ransomed in a bid to prevent her father signing an important treaty. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: When an embezzler returns from prison, a woman thought to be involved in his crime demands that he clear her name, but is soon accused of his murder

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A suspect in a drug-smuggling case is seemingly mauled to death by his own dog, which is immediately scheduled to be destroyed - but Abby refuses to hand it over to Navy officials and launches her own investigation. Meanwhile, Gibbs continues to worry about Shepard's health, convinced she and Ducky are hiding something. Drama, starring Pauley Perrette, Mark Harmon and Lauren Holly

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker's two-star rookies must track down a terrorist who threatens to unleash a deadly bioweapon on the city if the authorities do not meet his demands within 48 hours

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man and his son on a fishing trip hook what appears to be the body of a petty officer, but the dead man turns out to be a journalist who had been investigating the proliferation of drugs in the Navy. Meanwhile, Ziva returns to her car after a workout at the gym to find her father Eli hiding inside. Guest starring Michael Nouri

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. When examining CCTV footage of the incident, Sam and Callen discover the dead man tossed away a small object before jumping to his death, and when they learn the late lieutenant commander had been working on a sensitive project involving new cloaking technology for submarines, they immediately take an interest in the item. The investigation draws the team into the furtive world of LA's Chinese community, where they discover Lee might have been harbouring some personal as well as professional secrets. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents are investigated by the FBI on suspicion of murdering arms dealer La Grenouille. Jenny Shepard is the prime suspect, as she believed the criminal killed her father some years earlier, so Gibbs forms a plan to help his boss. However, an eyewitness later comes forward and implicates Tony. Starring Lauren Holly and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A naval officer involved in an illegal underground street race is killed when his vehicle explodes. Meanwhile, Callen latest driving misdemeanour means he must attend traffic school. Chris O'Donnell stars

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: CCTV footage proves Jay's girlfriend Cheryl was guilty of torching the home of troublesome lottery winners the Henderson family, but the team wonder whether the Sun Hill arsonist is still on the loose. Kezia sees a softer side to Smithy as he calmly solves a heated dispute and Lewis dreads living on his own when his gran announces she's going back to Jamaica. Sam tells ex-lovers Stuart and Phil she is to be their new boss

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Roger rescues probation officer Paul Brookes, who has been beaten and locked in the boot of his own car. Further investigations reveal the assailant's wife is a prisoner at Burnwood who claims she has been raped by Brookes. Gina turns up at court to give evidence, only to find the magistrate is an old flame, who promptly wipes the floor with her - before asking her out to dinner. Lewis finds a new home via the nurses' noticeboard at St Hugh's Hospital

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Heaton puts his team in the picture about the possible closure of Sun Hill when rumours reach the local press. Mickey and Jo investigate the death of a young mother with a history of drug use and discover she has been clean for some time but has fallen prey to a ruthless loan shark. Roger and Dan talk to a pub landlord who has been attacked at a fancy dress stag night

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: There's no turning back for Zain as he agrees to feed false information back to Sun Hill about the Colombian drug operation. Still wild about Kristen after their holiday together, he becomes indiscreet about his double life and rubs gangster Paul Haskew up the wrong way. Rob and Neil investigate a well-heeled man who appears to have assaulted his mother and Honey persuades Will to go away with her

programme.title: Oliver Twist
programme.desc: Part one of two. Adaptation of Charles Dickens' story. An orphan boy endures a horrible childhood in the workhouse, but yearns for more. After being sold to a cruel undertaker, he runs away to London - where he joins a group of pickpockets. Timothy Spall heads an all-star cast as Fagin, with support from Tom Hardy, Gregor Fisher, Sarah Lancashire, Sophie Okonedo, Julian Rhind-Tutt and William Miller in the title role

programme.title: Oliver Twist
programme.desc: Part two of two. Sikes escapes back to London with a badly wounded Oliver, while Mr Bumble declares his love for Mrs Corney - and captivated by the promise of social advancement, she agrees to marry him. Later, Nancy stumbles on a secret meeting, during which she overhears Monks telling Fagin he wants Oliver dead in two days

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Man Who Cried
programme.desc: Feature-length period drama about Abel Mason, an ordinary working man whose extramarital affair leads to murder, and prompts him to hit the road with his son in search of a new life. The pair head north and Abel eventually finds employment with a wealthy garage owner. Ciaran Hinds and Kate Buffery star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Winifred's eyes are opened to Poplar's seedy underworld when she cares for expectant prostitute Bridget Cole. Horrified by the young woman's lack of awareness, she draws on her teaching experience, and with help from Sgt Noakes sets out to promote safe sex to all local working girls. Barbara struggles to understand why expectant father Frank Robbins would pressure his wife to bear him a son, while Sister Monica Joan is feeling sidelined - until she finds herself unexpectedly assisting Shelagh with an emergency birth. Trixie invites herself along to a meeting between Tom and the Bishop - but things get tense when the idea of the couple moving to Newcastle is raised

programme.title: Les Misérables
programme.desc: Cosette has grown into a teenager, and to Valjean's dismay, questions her closeted life and is desperate for a new start in Paris. The opportunity presents itself when she meets Pontmercy's son Marius, who has turned his back on his family after learning the truth about his father, and now lives in a squalid tenement, where he catches the eye of the daughter of a criminal family. Javert ascends through the ranks of the police, and believes he is closing in on his goal

programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Henry does little to hide his disappointment when his queen gives birth to a girl, with Anne aware that her power in court rests on producing a male heir. With the Tudor line remaining vulnerable, Cromwell decrees that everyone in public life must take an oath to recognise Henry's supremacy as head of the English Church, as well as the legality of his marriage to Anne Boleyn, but Thomas More refuses to swear allegiance and is committed to the Tower. Mark Rylance, Claire Foy, Damian Lewis and Anton Lesser star

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: Part one of two. Walker is assigned to investigate the discovery of a young woman's skeleton, found underneath the patio of what used to be a boarding house, but is paired with Pat's ex-boyfriend Jeff. Drama from Widows creator Lynda LaPlante, starring David Hayman and Paul Kynman

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: A juggler seeks sanctuary at the abbey, desperate to escape an angry mob who believe he attacked and robbed a goldsmith. Sensing that events are not what they seem, Cadfael investigates - and it is not long before another body is found floating in the river. Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: Hugh faces a dilemma when his friend Joey makes a dying wish, and the Knight family support Ajax and Meryl as they each confront a crisis

programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Dorien takes her antique silver samovar to the Antiques Roadshow, inspiring the sisters to search for their own family heirlooms - and soon they make a surprising discovery

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Darryl is feeling the effects of two years in prison, prompting Dorien to offer her own brand of advice. Comedy, starring Alun Lewis and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: As the Serious Organised Crime Agency begin to question Zain's undercover role with the Colombian gang, drugs mule Eva Garcia contacts her brother, blowing the security of the safe house and putting the detective in great danger from kingpin Alvarez. Honey argues with Will about pressing him to go on holiday together but before they can sort out their relationship she answers an emergency call from Eva

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Annalese suspects Michael is being unfaithful and rummages through his belongings, hunting for evidence. Ric refuses to treat an obese man as part of his zero tolerance policy, but promises Connie he does not intend to stay long as director of surgery. Daisha's friends throw her a surprise birthday party, and Joseph continues to struggle with his guilt over his night with Jac

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Tensions are running high as the day of Baz's funeral dawns, and despite Charlie and Dan's best efforts to keep their antipathy under control for Louis's sake, it seems inevitable that strong feelings will boil over. Meanwhile, Lara decides to risk everything and challenge Harry, while Jim receives shocking news about Andy

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jack and Soca Officer Marcus Swift follow the false lead and prepare to corner the drugs gang at Jamaica Dock, while newcomer Honey is being held prisoner at Bonham Wharf boatyard, hoping in vain for help from her colleagues. Haskew finally convinces Kristen that Zain really is an undercover cop, and Jack is horrified to learn his detective has been sleeping with the sultry club owner

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: It's a busy day for Dr Legg as a mystery virus spreads. Cindy crashes into a car after seeing Sam and David hugging

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Robbie asks Peggy about dating. Ian is determined to win the pub quiz. Bianca has food poisoning but David and Ricky argue about who should look after her

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Elizabeth decides to organise a formal party to demonstrate how out of her depth Sarah is. Meanwhile, Carolyn is shocked by Jack's revelation when she challenges him to confront his intimacy issues, and Regina returns home from Europe with news about Sarah. Romantic drama, starring Marta Dusseldorp

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Will comes to the aid of a feuding couple, helping them to see that life's too short for petty squabbles, while Alex has to summon up the courage of her convictions as a Down's syndrome patient presents her with a moral dilemma. Simon Shepherd and Maggie O'Neill star

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James visits the dog track dash, but the greyhound owners consider him a touch too conscientious when his services are called upon. Tristan stirs up trouble when he tries his hand at cooking. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Jeffrey adds another string to his bow by filling in for the local vicar. Campsite comedy, starring Simon Cadell, Jeffrey Holland, Ruth Madoc and Paul Shane

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: The day of the annual Yellowcoat competition dawns - and the stakes are raised when a Caribbean holiday is up for grabs. Comedy, starring Su Pollard and Paul Shane

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth discovers Emmet has planned a piano concert for the senior citizens at the church hall, and decides to treat the lucky audience to her own brand of sing-song. Suburban comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and David Griffin

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Chaos abounds at an annual parade held to celebrate a famous battle. The lifelike effigy of the old General has been spruced up and Nora Batty prepares to sit on a float playing the part of his wife. Norman Wisdom makes a cameo appearance

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The green-fingered sleuths are hired to work behind the scenes of a garden makeover TV show, but Rosemary has an accident while standing in for the programme's star and ends up in hospital. Realising someone is trying to sabotage the series, Laura teams up with no-nonsense presenter Quinnie Dorell (Belinda Lang) to catch the culprit. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: The private detective tells the police that the circumstances of Rochelle's death merit further investigation, but is dismissed. He then seeks out Lula's friends and learns that she was looking for her biological family, before visiting the Bristow family home and meeting Lady Yvette, who reveals that she and John were watching a film when Lula died. Meanwhile, more of the Strike's personal history is revealed to Robin when his half-sister Lucy invades the office

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: While Sasha is occupied with a training course, the boys' investigations take them to a lookalike agency, where their top two acts were found dead in an apparent double suicide 13 years ago. In an industry reliant on the art of the illusion and their suspects intent on giving them the run around, Ted, Steve and Danny are convinced there's more to this case than first meets the eye. Steve is thrilled when their enquiries take them to the door of one of his football heroes, but his glee is short-lived. Detective drama, starring Denis Lawson

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: The failure of another attempt to limit Iran's nuclear threat proves all the other nations have their own agendas and operate by their own sets of rules. Adam kills a mugger who turns out to be a journalist investigating the Tehran bombing, and his day gets even worse when it becomes clear Ana has been compromised. Meanwhile, Yalta tests Ros's loyalties further

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jack and Soca Officer Marcus Swift follow the false lead and prepare to corner the drugs gang at Jamaica Dock, while newcomer Honey is being held prisoner at Bonham Wharf boatyard, hoping in vain for help from her colleagues. Haskew finally convinces Kristen that Zain really is an undercover cop, and Jack is horrified to learn his detective has been sleeping with the sultry club owner

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Change is on the way to the sleepy market town of Cranford as the railway pushes out from Manchester, but many of the female residents are more excited about the arrival of the handsome new doctor, Frank Harrison. Adapted by Heidi Thomas, with an all-star cast including Judi Dench and Eileen Atkins, who was awarded a Bafta for her role as the authoritative Miss Deborah Jenkyns

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: The town's residents prepare for Lady Ludlow's garden party, but the occasion is marred by rumours the railway line will come right into Cranford, leaving Deborah feeling betrayed. Dr Harrison's plans to become better acquainted with Sophy are thwarted by Caroline Tomkinson and Clara Smith, Mr Carter takes pity on an illiterate 10-year-old and Miss Matty is shaken to see a face from her past. Starring Judi Dench, Michael Gambon and Eileen Atkins

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: An encounter with an old flame makes Sharon feel broody, while Tracey has her work cut out pampering Garth in the school holidays. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Part one of two. Harry puts his colleagues' lives at risk during an underground bonding exercise after ignoring the marked route and slipping down a shaft. As the hole floods, Simon volunteers to swim underwater to an opening, but in a moment of confusion knocks his head against a rock and fails to resurface. Charlie presents Dan with a court order to stop him taking Louis out of the country

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A Friday night shift proves a hectic time for the Sun Hill coppers as a childhood friend of Amber's is raped, a drug-dealer is collared and a mugging victim is attacked for a second time - and in the aftermath of all the excitement, Phil's unruly hormones are fired by an encounter with a fellow sex addict. A guilt-ridden Honey decides to tell Steve their relationship is over, Neil pours out his heart to Andrea, and Yvonne learns she's no longer Acting Sergeant - leading to much speculation about her replacement

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Michelle gets a surprise at work while Nigel braves out Debbie's inquest. Alan and Carol find a safe haven

programme.title: Classic EastEnders

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Will makes the professional mistake of getting emotionally attached to a patient with psychiatric problems, and a black cloud descends over Kerri and Sam's engagement party. Medical drama, starring Simon Shepherd, John Duttine and Denise Black

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Siegfried investigates when an outbreak of dog poisoning sweeps through the district, while Tristan joins the local bellringers for a farewell drink. Rural drama, starring Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Joe Maplin demotes Gladys and replaces her with a new supervisor, who requires her own chalet - throwing the accommodation situation into chaos. Comedy, starring Simon Cadell and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Barry and Yvonne tread the light fantastic, and Spike has a humorous conversation with Pinocchio, while Ted attempts to foil Joe Maplin's expansion plans. Comedy, starring Paul Shane

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Daddy dresses up in a spaceman suit and goes on the rampage in the toy department of a local shop, forcing an embarrassed Hyacinth to intervene. Suburban comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Lionel has a habit of being unpredictable, but when he walks off and abandons his wife while out on a drive in the hills, it is left to Clegg, Truly and Billy to console her. Long-running comedy set in Yorkshire, from the pen of Roy Clarke. Roy Hudd guest stars

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode of the mystery drama. The sleuthing senior citizen is urged by an old friend to visit her sister as she is convinced something is wrong. Miss Marple heads for the country to investigate and it is not long before a heated argument in a locked room ends with a gunshot that leaves a man dead. Jean Simmons and Joss Ackland guest star, with Joan Hickson

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: UCOS looks into the murder of a private investigator who was stabbed through the heart in a suspected robbery gone wrong. The answers may lie with the victim's last three cases, but it'll take the whole team's expertise to crack the codes within his mysterious notebook. The movements of a rare stamp could hold the key to the investigation - if only UCOS could track it down. Meanwhile, Steve has problems when he rents out his flat

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: When Ros's bugs inform Yalta that Harry intends to ground all planes to prevent a nuclear warhead circuit board reaching Iran, she and Adam go undercover on an airline flying to the country intending to identify the courier. Meanwhile, Jo and Malcolm must stop the poisoning of London's water supply, as threatened by terrorists seeking to guarantee that Iran has nuclear capability

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A Friday night shift proves a hectic time for the Sun Hill coppers as a childhood friend of Amber's is raped, a drug-dealer is collared and a mugging victim is attacked for a second time - and in the aftermath of all the excitement, Phil's unruly hormones are fired by an encounter with a fellow sex addict. A guilt-ridden Honey decides to tell Steve their relationship is over, Neil pours out his heart to Andrea, and Yvonne learns she's no longer Acting Sergeant - leading to much speculation about her replacement

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Christmas arrives in the town, but Lady Ludlow regrets any notion of seasonal charity when Mr Carter asks her to intervene in the case of Job Gregson, who has been blamed for a spate of thefts. Miss Matty is delighted when Mr Holbrook invites her to his farm, and Dr Harrison's friend Jack sends Caroline Tomkinson a card hinting she should expect a proposal from the medic. Starring Judi Dench, Michael Gambon and Philip Glenister

programme.title: Cranford
programme.desc: Miss Matty confides in Mary about Mr Holbrook's death, prompting her to inform Major Gordon of Jessie's regret over not marrying him. Dr Harrison tells Rev Hutton of his intentions toward Sophy and asks permission to court her, blissfully unaware that the generosity he has shown to Mrs Rose has accidentally created an unlikely love triangle. Penultimate episode of the costume drama, starring Judi Dench and Simon Woods

programme.desc: Footage of marine life in different environments around the north, beginning with a tropical coral reef which has medicinal properties for dolphins, and is used as a tool by tusk fish. programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison explores the powers of the great outdoors to provide a tonic in troubled times as she immerses herself in nature, from forest bathing to wild swimming. There are also films from the Countryfile archives about the health-giving prowess of the UK's green havens, including John Craven finding out how nature helped heal wartime wounds, and Anita Rani visiting a special farm giving respite to refugees

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from Saturday's performances. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and guest judge Anton Du Beke decide who will be the third pair to leave the contest. With music by the Vamps

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra crosses into Will's world and finds it to be very different to her own, and after encountering the enigmatic Charles Latrom, the pair head to find a scholar to help get answers about Dust. Will is shocked to discover he has grandparents, but quickly realises he can't trust them, while a calculating Mrs Coulter pushes MacPhail to act after Graves' strong performance during the interrogation of a witch consul

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: The first episode of Steve McQueen's drama anthology based on the real-life experiences of London's West Indian community from the late-1960s to the mid-1980s. The film tells the story of the Old Bailey trial of Frank Crichlow, the owner of West Indian restaurant Mangrove in London's Notting Hill, who in 1970 was one of nine men and women wrongly arrested and charged with incitement to riot and affray after a protest about racial discrimination by the police. Starring Shaun Parkes, Letitia Wright and Malachi Kirby

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Manchester United v Manchester City and Aston Villa v Birmingham City

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the stories of properties in Nuneaton, Wolverhampton and Stoke that are being transformed before being sold at auction

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: CCTV footage reveals the truth when opportunists claim for injuries after being hit by a bus, and false claims of a serious injury sustained on a running machine are exposed

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A woman gives birth on the M5 with the help of her husband and an emergency call handler, while a family's car is hit by an oak tree during a storm

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and David Harper help two teams look for items at the Newark Antiques Fair in Nottinghamshire, while Charlie Ross finds out about the legacy of a local woman

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma confronts Zara over her betrayal, and Al embarrasses Valerie at their cookery class. Jimmi helps an ex-offender struggling to adjust to modern life

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson catches up with a couple who bought a home in North Yorkshire, before Nicki Chapman enjoys a reunion with a couple who were seeking a property in the Welsh Borders

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from a recycling centre in Witley, then she and two experts transform a nursing chair, a coffee table and some vintage bottles

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Margie Cooper start their hunt in the west of Scotland, buying items including a model of a Scottish terrier and a a Robert Burns-themed object to sell at auction in Dumfries

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas presents the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In-depth current affairs report covering a story behind the headlines

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Kat feels nervous about the robbery and with tensions escalating between her and Shirley, she tells the Mitchells the job is off. Can Ben convince her to go through with it? Denise tries to find the courage to tell Raymond she is his birth mother, aware it will come as a shock to the youngster - but she is the one in for a surprise. Tina discovers she has outstayed her welcome

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedian James Acaster, TV presenters Gabby Logan and Melvin Odoom and Cold Feet star Fay Ripley join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show in which participants attempt to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: My Family, the Holocaust & Me with Robert Rinder
programme.desc: Part two of two. The barrister helps Noemie Lopian find out how her French mother survived the war after she was arrested as a child by the Nazis, meeting the grandson of the man who hid her mother and siblings in a chicken shed in his garden during round-ups of Jews in the town of Saint-Junien. Noemie also learns of the incredible sacrifice and courage of a young female French resistance fighter who saved her mother's life

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: The salon juniors find out which of them are getting promoted, things step up between Sammyjo and her crush, and there is surprise for Bev as one Longlox girl has some big news

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: Filmed across four days as the Covid-19 pandemic develops, Sammyjo and Bev deal with professional and personal challenges leading up to their biggest decision yet

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fifth episode, married couple Dan and Alison from Swansea face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell visit a former bank and a two-bed semi in Lancashire, and a one bedroom flat with a garage in Levenshulme, Manchester

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: CCTV footage proves to be the undoing of a con man claiming he cannot work due to an injury, and in America, a doctor abuses his position to commit medical health insurance fraud

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A doctor and a paramedic try to save the life of a hiker who fell 650ft, while a motorist takes evasive action when a car heads towards him on the wrong side of the road

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning presents from Leominster, Herefordshire, where Gary Pe and John Cameron are the experts assisting the two sides. Plus, Anita shows off pieces of pottery with a royal connection

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma's suspension hits the local press with an unexpected headline, the Pitman Telegraph is put to the test, and a test result turns the life of a Baking Vlogger upside down

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson and Ginny Buckley catch up with an adventurous couple with three huskies who relocated to Derbyshire and lifelong city dwellers who moved to the West Sussex coast

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jacqui Joseph gets creative with a mannequin at Earlswood Recycling Centre, while restorers Neil Wragg and Rachel Henderson get to work on some sofa leather and an old metal trunk

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Margie Cooper and Natasha Raskin Sharp hit the antique shops of Scotland, where they find an unusual pyjama case and a rare piece of Victoriana to sell at auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ben, Kush and Phil head to the job, while Shirley and Kat create a diversion and act as lookout. Can they pull it off? Callum is caught between a rock and a hard place, while Max has an idea to help Linda, making Sharon suspicious. With nowhere to go, Tina asks Kathy if she can stay with her, while Suki offers Ian a deal

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac rushes in a mystery patient, whose arrival sets in motion in a deepening medical crisis that could have serious repercussions for her secret relationship with Kian. Keller is shocked when a new member of staff reveals the truth about Sacha's deception, and is tempted to take out his anguish on a vulnerable patient. Cameron basks in the glory of being declared a hero - prompting Nicky to issue him with an ultimatum

programme.title: The Crash Detectives
programme.desc: When calls start coming in to report debris on the M4, police discover that a father and son have been in a serious crash. As traffic chaos builds, the pressure is on for the collision team to find out why the car left the road

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Marcus Waring challenges two of today's four chefs to butcher a chicken, debone the thighs and cook them with asparagus and a brandy, mushroom and mustard cream sauce, all in just 20 minutes. The others are set a task by Monica Galetti, and must create a fruit tart filled with a berry compote, piped pistachio chantilly and candied pistachios

programme.title: Ovie: Life After Reality TV
programme.desc: Professional basketball player and Love Island star Ovie Soko decides which direction to take his life, as he explores the good and the bad of the reality TV industry.

programme.title: My Family, the Holocaust & Me with Robert Rinder
programme.desc: Part two of two. The barrister helps Noemie Lopian find out how her French mother survived the war after she was arrested as a child by the Nazis, meeting the grandson of the man who hid her mother and siblings in a chicken shed in his garden during round-ups of Jews in the town of Saint-Junien. Noemie also learns of the incredible sacrifice and courage of a young female French resistance fighter who saved her mother's life

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter Q, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter R, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith visit Cornwall, heading to Falmouth for the start of the oyster season, where successful hatching is down to the moon. They also discover how old traditions are being used in modern ways, from boatbuilding to using horses to manage an ancient landscape, while Charlotte helps out in the exotic gardens of Trelissick. Adam Henson finds out why British turkey farmers could be in for a tough time this Christmas.

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond visits the Abriachan Forest by Loch Ness, one of the first community woodlands in Scotland. Euan McIlwraith is in Moray to investigate the worrying increase in incidents of hare coursing and Arlene Stuart finds out why there is a problem with the provision of public toilets across the country

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Looking at the physical and mental benefits of gardening, including a visit to a unique community growing project in Dundee combating poverty and giving escape from addiction

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: Born in 1962 to Nigerian parents studying medicine in London, Kemi Nevins now runs a successful cafe in Abercynon serving up delicious food. However, none of that she offers reflects her Nigerian background. Now Kemi's children are adults themselves, they would like to know more about their heritage, so the Welsh-Italian cook visits Kemi to help her reconnect to her roots through her love of food

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate British beef, using it in a suet pudding and making oxtail soup. They visit a farmer who is focusing on the future with his wagyu cattle, discover the history of the much-maligned dish tripe and learn how to roast a whole ox

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Setting out on his horticultural tour, Monty Don finds a tasteful blend of ancient and modern landscaping in the antiquated water gardens of Mexico City, and the post-modern surreal fantasies of Luis Barragan in the Mexican rainforest. On the Caribbean island of Cuba, the intrepid gardener discovers a crumbling urban landscape playing host to a budding natural revolution

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of day one from The O2 in London, where the opening singles round-robin match takes place. The season-ending tournament will see the top eight available singles players in the world compete for this prestigious title, which was won last year by Stefanos Tsitsipas when he defeated Dominic Thiem in the final

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charlie Ross and Will Axon assess items at Ragley Hall in Warwickshire, including a Busby hat and a Worcester coffee set. Presenter Paul Martin explores the Battle of Edgehill

programme.title: 633 Squadron (1964)
programme.desc: British airmen embark on a daring mission to destroy a heavily fortified German rocket-fuel plant in Norway, and to ensure success the RAF pilots have to bomb an overhanging cliff to collapse it onto the building. The plan threatens to unravel, however, when a Norwegian freedom fighter plotting a land attack to coincide with the air strike is captured by the Nazis. Second World War drama, starring Cliff Robertson, George Chakiris, Donald Houston and Harry Andrews

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The RNLI volunteers at Dunmore East in the Irish Sea are called to a motorboat stranded several miles out and taking on water, a man is caught under a pier in the Thames and two boys in north Wales get caught in a treacherous current chasing an errant football

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: An inventor with a passion for pets hopes Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Jenny Campbell and Tej Lalvani will appreciate his cat whistle, while an ice-cream entrepreneur plans to tempt the multi-millionaires to invest in her coconut-based desserts. Plus, a carpenter brings the outside indoors for some fun and games

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the conclusion of his two-part journey, Simon Reeve investigates the environmental challenges facing Cornwall and the rest of the country. He discovers how the region's farmers are adopting new, less intensive approaches that are both more profitable and environmentally friendly, and reveals how reintroducing beavers to the uplands could tackle the problem of widespread flooding. He also encounters some of the county's iconic wildlife, including grey seals that live in constant risk of getting caught up in discarded fishing nets

programme.title: The Trials of Oscar Pistorius
programme.desc: Documentary series focusing on the South African former Paralympic star, who was convicted for the murder of his girlfriend Reeva Steenkamp on Valentine's Day in 2013. A month after the shooting, Oscar Pistorius was released on bail, and moved into his uncle's compound. His legal team hired a forensic expert to recreate his version of events

programme.title: Golf: The Masters
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the fourth and final day of the Major, which took place at Augusta National in Augusta, Georgia. Tiger Woods won this title for the first time since 2005 last year, finishing one stroke clear of American compatriots Dustin Johnson, Brooks Koepka and Xander Schauffele. With commentary by Peter Allis, Ken Brown, Andrew Cotter and Iain Carter

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: The mystery surrounding Peter's appearance in Ireland is revealed in an episode exploring the events of the past four months. Nathan, Niki and DL's back stories are recalled, while Maya and Alejandro go on the run from the authorities. Milo Ventimiglia, Adrian Pasdar and Dania Ramirez star

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Noah Bennet causes an argument with Claire when he prepares to move his family because the Company has found them. Matt attempts to master his new mental abilities, while Hiro returns to the present to find out his father is dead - so he travels back in time to prevent his murder. Masi Oka and Hayden Panettiere star

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric wakes up from his coma to find the world in the middle of the Covid-19 pandemic - and that the ongoing crisis threatens his chance of surviving his recent brain surgery. Sacha's colleagues are concerned by how he is taking Essie's death, but he turns his back on them in favour of a stranger, while Cameron struggles to prove that he can be a hero

programme.title: Between the Covers
programme.desc: Sara Cox invites Laura Whitmore, Anita Rani, Russell Kane and Alan Davies to discuss the books that take them back to their youth, the books that transport them to far-flung places and the books that make them feel positive about the world. They also talk about this week's book group picks - Small Pleasures by Clare Chambers and Alan Davies' new book Just Ignore Him

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson returns to the Isle of Wight, the location of a previous property hunt, wanting to discover whether a couple he helped succeeded in finding a balance of home and business potential in their new setting. Back on the mainland, Jules travels to south-west Wales to meet a husband and wife who craved a sizable Welsh farmhouse, but who have found that a home is about so much more than bricks and mortar

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Presenter Jay Blades enlists the help of designer Sarah Peterson to give two side tables a stylish new look, while lighting expert Duncan McKean attempts to give two old record players a radical overhaul. For his own project, Jay attempts to make some money for nothing from two farmhouse stools. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: A look at the work of mountain vets at the ancient Kingdom of Mourne, Northern Ireland. Cahir is racing to an urgent call-out from a local pedigree sheep breeder, whose prize ewe is having difficulty in labour, and has to perform an emergency C-section. Nuala diagnoses a deep-rooted infection, finding her only choice is to put the calf down or take on a challenging amputation. Meanwhile, Jennie sees a newborn lamb brought to the clinic by its dedicated young owner, farmer Stephen

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary Berry explores the world of simple comfort food, demonstrating how to make heartwarming, fuss-free dishes that can warm the cockles and lift the spirits. In the opening episode, Mary heads back to the city where she learnt to cook, Paris, where she visits one of the city's creperies to finesse her technique. Later, inspired by pastries in Parisian patisserie windows, she makes her own brioche and apple frangipane tart

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: 800 Words
programme.desc: It is the eve of Woody and Tracy's wedding, so not a great time for a power cut across the whole town, but everyone pulls together to make it a day to remember

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode's alphabetical journey through the BBC's culinary archive continues as he reaches the letter K. Highlights include Rick Stein cooking lamb koftas and Raymond Blanc serving up kougelhopf

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of day two from The O2 in London, where a singles round-robin match takes place. Eventual champion Stefanos Tsitsipas got off to a winning start in this session last year, prevailing 7-6, 6-4 against Daniil Medvedev

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores nature's evolutionary anomalies, beginning by examining how the unique features of the chameleon and the giraffe have benefited them in their respective environments. He then looks at curious creatures that have led to accusations of forgery, such as the duck-billed platypus and the midwife toad

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Revisiting some of the historic venues from the series, as well as new valuations and unexpected auctions. Paul Martin uncovers the history of the border town Berwick-upon-Tweed

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angela Barnes, Melvin Odoom, Greg Rutherford and Denise Van Outen test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball is joined by the third couple to be eliminated from the competition after losing the dance-off at the weekend, and there are interviews with the remaining pairs

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair to answer questions on the specialist subjects of he Weimar Republic, Alex Higgins, the bridges of London, and UK cheeses, before each is tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts as two teams who lost their first-round matches get another chance to stay in the competition and reach the second stage. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues comprising Black suits, White meat, Blue corner, and Green light

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares recipes that can be made in advance for cheesecake ice-cream and a fish stew, as well as a fried chicken sandwich garnished with some of her favourite condiments. Nigella also makes a simple yet daring dish of cherry flambé using frozen fruit and kirsch, and uses up the final slices of a soft white loaf to prepare rum-spiked French toast

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: It is mid-summer and the Royal Free London NHS Hospital Foundation Trust faces the long-lasting effects of Covid-19, with departments across the trust battling for scarce theatre slots while trying to prioritise their volume of cases. At Barnet Hospital, a large team meeting is underway when a clinician is informed he has tested positive for coronavirus and must leave the site immediately, while the infection control team springs into action to identify who has had 'significant contact' with him and must therefore also go home and isolate for 14 days

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon is joined by Clara Amfo and Dev Griffin to take a light-hearted look at the year's cultural highlights, including the first series of Strictly Come Dancing and the final episodes of Friends and Sex and the City

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark

programme.title: World Cup 1966: Alfie's Boys
programme.desc: David Jason presents a documentary about how Alf Ramsey recruited the England football team that won the world cup in 1966, and was able to unite the members into a world class side. Featuring archive footage of the team in action and contributions from Bobby Charlton, Jack Charlton, Jimmy Greaves, George Cohen, Tina Moore, Harry Redknapp, Terry Venables, Geoff Hurst and Gordon Banks

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith visit Cornwall, heading to Falmouth for the start of the oyster season, where successful hatching is down to the moon. They also discover how old traditions are being used in modern ways, from boatbuilding to using horses to manage an ancient landscape, while Charlotte helps out in the exotic gardens of Trelissick. Adam Henson finds out why British turkey farmers could be in for a tough time this Christmas.

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: Incident Response Officer Andy is dispatched when a call comes in about a reported stabbing, while Adam and Orani are dispatched to treat a long-term heroin addict whose concerned mother has called the ambulance service for help. Elsewhere, Kieran and Mick are dispatched to their second patient of the night, an 82-year-old lady who has fallen at home and has been waiting for an ambulance for two hours

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: The team tracks the changes in nature throughout autumn, with Chris Packham in the New Forest watching how it transforms the area near his home, while Michaela Strachan is at a NatureScot reserve in Fife to report on a giant grey seal breeding colony on the nearby Isle of May

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson catches up with a couple who bought a home in North Yorkshire, before Nicki Chapman enjoys a reunion with a couple who were seeking a property in the Welsh Borders

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from a recycling centre in Witley, then she and two experts transform a nursing chair, a coffee table and some vintage bottles

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: The Welsh-Italian cook heads to Snowdonia to hear the real-life Romeo and Juliet story of Vincenzo Algieri and Betty Evans - two star-crossed lovers who, despite being on opposite sides of the Second World War, found love together against all the odds. From different cultures and speaking different languages, Vincenzo and Betty bonded over food - traditional Italian dishes the family still treasures to this day. Grandson Huw has some clues of the tale the lies within his family history, but hopes Michela's specialist knowledge can help fill in some of the blanks

programme.title: Powering Britain
programme.desc: Keeley Donovan meets the army of people working to meet the country's energy demands. She begins 75 miles off the Yorkshire coast at the world's biggest offshore windfarm. Hornsea One has broken every record for its size and scale - with 10,000 people involved in its construction in the North Sea. Keeley meets some of the staff who made it happen - but can she get to the top of one of the 174 turbines in the field?

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including St Helens v Warrington Wolves, Hull FC v Wigan Warriors and Huddersfield Giants v Catalans Dragons

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk and Anna Haugh prepare George V's favourite curry, Polly Russell discovers how Francis Drake solved a debt crisis and the secrets of a famous condiment are revealed

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP World Tour Finals
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents coverage of day three from The O2 in London, where a singles round-robin match takes place. Six-time champion Roger Federer overcame Matteo Berrettini 7-6, 6-3 in this session last year, but eventually failed to qualify for the final

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the vision in squid and owls, revealing how these entirely different creatures have both adapted to life in the dark. He also looks at gorillas and vampire bats both gained unpleasant reputations within the animal kingdom

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: James Lewis and Jonathan Pratt are in Glemham Hall in Suffolk, where they uncover a 17th-century apothecary jar, while Paul Martin learns about a medieval port lost to the sea

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angela Barnes, Melvin Odoom, Greg Rutherford and Denise Van Outen go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball chats to more couples about their training progress. Plus, a closer look at the weekend's performances, as well as more backstage gossip

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedian Russell Kane and presenter AJ Odudu navigate their way around Shropshire looking for antiques, accompanied by experts Phil Serrell and Margie Cooper. Eventually they head to an auction to discover whose haul raises the most money

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Volunteers come to the aid of a family trapped in their car on the causeway connecting the Holy Island of Lindisfarne to the mainland, who face being slowly engulfed by the sea. The crew of one of the RNLI's few inland stations in Ireland assists two capsized kayakers just a few days after Christmas, and on Dorset's Jurassic Coast, a fossil hunter is cut off by a rising tide

programme.title: QI XS
programme.desc: A selection of highlights from the comedy panel game, in which Sandi Toksvig takes on the subject of travel, accompanied by Alan Davies and their guests

programme.title: Industry
programme.desc: The company tries to move on in the wake of the recent tragedy. Yasmin's relationship with her boyfriend hits rock bottom, but flirting with Robert and a surprising game of cat and mouse provide a welcome distraction. Harper is desperate to get out of her temporary accommodation and away from the bad memories - and receives help from a surprising source

programme.title: Out of Her Mind
programme.desc: Sara's father Lewis is back in her life, and his return forces her to re-examine all her important male relationships. She tries to dig deeper into why Lewis and her fiancé both left her. Guest starring Adrian Edmondson

programme.title: Mandy
programme.desc: It is dinner at Trent Manor, and everyone around the table has a motive for murdering Lord Trent. But who did? Mandy wasn't invited to the manor, so she is off doing something else. Comedy short, written, directed by and starring Diane Morgan, with guest star David Bradley

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Kirsty Wark

programme.title: Cornwall with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the conclusion of his two-part journey, Simon Reeve investigates the environmental challenges facing Cornwall and the rest of the country. He discovers how the region's farmers are adopting new, less intensive approaches that are both more profitable and environmentally friendly, and reveals how reintroducing beavers to the uplands could tackle the problem of widespread flooding. He also encounters some of the county's iconic wildlife, including grey seals that live in constant risk of getting caught up in discarded fishing nets

programme.title: Life in Lockdown: A Special Film for BBC Children in Need
programme.desc: A powerful, uplifting and touching film which proves how brilliantly resilient children and young people can be in the face of a global crisis. This film shines a light on children and young people experiencing a variety of challenges whilst they also cope with the effects of the pandemic, and reveals how BBC Children in Need funded projects are providing vital support for families during this incredibly difficult time. Narrated by Emma Willis

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: Harry Hill focuses on the history of home improvement shows. From Do It Yourself with Barry Bucknell to Grand Designs, Harry reveals the tricks of the trade when making these shows

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams all look at the unseen side of autumn from their four separate locations across the country. Tonight, Michaela shares more drama from the Scottish seal colony, and Chris reveals the latest antics of the multi-coloured badger family

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the wildlife of the US state, including footage of manatees, baby alligators and miniature deer living in Florida's pine forests, coral reefs and famous Everglades wetlands. The programme considers the dangers facing the animal population, including natural disasters like wildfires, flooding and hurricanes, as well as the pressure of a growing human presence in their territory

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob explores the musical heritage of New Orleans, and considers what the future holds for the city in the aftermath of Hurricane Katrina. He talks to local performers Aaron Neville, Irma Thomas and Clarence Henry, who are determined to return to their home town, while Paul McCartney, Jools Holland, Dr John and Elvis Costello discuss how the city's musicians have inspired their work

programme.title: Ronnie's: Ronnie Scott & His World-Famous Jazz Club
programme.desc: The history of Ronnie Scott's Jazz Club, eponymously named after the late tenor saxophonist who founded the establishment with business partner Pete King. It opened in London's Soho in October 1959, inspired by the vibrant post-war jazz venues in New York. Since then, Ronnie's has consistently played host to many of the world's greatest jazz legends, including Chet Baker, Count Basie, Miles Davies, Ella Fitzgerald, Buddy Rich and Nina Simone who are all featured in the film. Contributors include Ronnie's friends, family and the music elite, including Chris Blackwell, Quincy Jones and Sonny Rollins

programme.title: Ella Fitzgerald at Ronnie Scott's
programme.desc: A 1974 performance at the London club by the American jazz singer, including Sweet Georgia Brown, It Don't Mean a Thing (If It Ain't Got That Swing) and Swingin' the Blues

programme.title: Jazz Horns Gold
programme.desc: A selection of archive performances by jazz artists from shows including Jazz 625, The Late Show, Later - with Jools Holland and Crackerjack. Featuring Dizzy Gillespie, Coleman Hawkins, Stan Getz, Rahsaan Roland Kirk, Wynton Marsalis, Acker Bilk, John Dankworth, Courtney Pine, John Surman and Andy Hamilton. Plus, the sounds of jazz disciples of the 21st century such as Joshua Redman and Hypnotic Brass Ensemble

programme.title: 21st Century Mythologies with Richard Clay
programme.desc: The art historian explores some of the everyday myths of 21st-century popular culture, revealing the hidden meanings in everything from money, Wi-fi and race to the Madonna. Along the way, he meets avant-garde artists including Clet Abraham, Ingrid Burrington, Molly Soda and Rene Matic, and looks at semiotics, the study of signs and symbols that provides an analytical toolkit to helps navigate advertising and its demands for attention

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob explores the musical heritage of New Orleans, and considers what the future holds for the city in the aftermath of Hurricane Katrina. He talks to local performers Aaron Neville, Irma Thomas and Clarence Henry, who are determined to return to their home town, while Paul McCartney, Jools Holland, Dr John and Elvis Costello discuss how the city's musicians have inspired their work

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the wildlife of the US state, including footage of manatees, baby alligators and miniature deer living in Florida's pine forests, coral reefs and famous Everglades wetlands. The programme considers the dangers facing the animal population, including natural disasters like wildfires, flooding and hurricanes, as well as the pressure of a growing human presence in their territory

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Building of Britain
programme.desc: Fred uncovers the construction techniques of stonemasons and carpenters through the ages, focusing on Norman cathedrals. The former steeplejack admires Peterborough Cathedral and Rochester Castle, where he demonstrates how tactics used to invade the fortress centuries ago resemble today's demolition methods

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a majestic stand of evergreen trees overlooking sweet tiny flowers growing on a sloping knoll

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Social historian Emma Dabiri and Dr Bendor Grosvenor travel to Glasgow to investigate a long-lost picture of George Villiers, Duke of Buckingham, who was the lover of James VI of Scotland, possibly painted by one of the most famous artists in history

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary telling the dramatic story of the devastating fire at the Paris cathedral on April 15, 2019, following the firefighters who faced the responsibility of saving one of the city's most emblematic and much-loved symbols from burning to the ground

programme.title: Rebuilding Notre Dame: Inside the Great Cathedral Rescue
programme.desc: Following efforts to rebuild the world-famous Cathedral of Notre Dame de Paris, a year on from the devastating fire that ravaged the 850-year-old Gothic masterpiece. Chief architect Philippe Villeneuve urgently needs a complete picture of the damage sustained during the fire, so he initiates an unprecedented collaboration between architects and scientists. Their mission is to meticulously analyse the fallen timber, stone and fractured glass, to develop a decontamination and restoration plan. This unique opportunity offers a new insight into the medieval materials, techniques and people who built Notre Dame

programme.title: How to Build a Cathedral
programme.desc: Architectural historian Jon Cannon explores how cathedrals were constructed, looking at fan vaulting at Gloucester, stained glass at York, and the Octagon at Ely for examples of medieval ingenuity, showing how they were built to realise a vision of Heaven on Earth

programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po are outside in the cold when they spot the Tiddlytubbies who are warm, inside. The Teletubbies put on hats, gloves and scarves to keep warm

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog Loves Bedtime Baboon? Dog and Pug go on an adventure to help Bedtime Baboon.

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Ferne Corrigan meets up with Jayden and Skyla at a city fame in London, where a vet is visiting to check the goats. Meanwhile, Rory Crawford observes an elephant receiving veterinary attention at a safari park

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Maia goes on a woodland walk with her family. Before they go, she draws up a list of all the things she wants to see and packs a camera, binoculars and picnic

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie learns about clouds and rain and discovers how a radiator is made

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Captain Captain knits some new uniforms for the crew

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The dog and the Squirrels go off on an underwater adventure with Betty's dad in his submarine, spotting creatures including a giant sea stallion and some jazz fish

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Mr McGregor locks Peter in a cage with Sammy Whiskers and he must join forces with the rat to escape

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: In this episode, CBeebies’ very own motorbike rider, Grace, shows off three amazing machines that all belong in the swamp. First Grace goes for a ride in an airboat and shows off an unusual corkscrew-propelled machine, and then she goes for a drive in a swamp buggy.

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Disaster strikes at Safari World when Andy accidentally breaks a bowerbird nest that Mr Hammond was going to put on display

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Princess Purl and Princess Bitz have decided to create the best party ever for Sir Robo Bob and Sir Bevel

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: After playing with DIY Derek's dog at home, Topsy and Mum go to Grandma's house, where Topsy examines her dog Mossy using her toy vet set and cheers her up with a tennis ball

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle is awarded the class toy for good behaviour, and Zoe and Callum have big news

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo needs to find different-coloured leaves to complete her nature tick-book, but she can’t find a green one. What will she do? Luckily, Gran Gran has a Gran Gran Plan that involves a special leaf rubbing!

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah suggests visiting the spare sounds section of the Big Shop when Duck loses his quack

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Mr Onion is playing quietly with his toy bridge when he is interrupted by Little Nana and Lambkin, playing a noisy hopping game

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pontipines go to meet the Wottingers, but they are out

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Katie Piper reads Where Happiness Lives, a tale about discovering true happiness and appreciating all that a person has

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: Andrew's guests this morning include U?ur ?ahin, BioNTech founder, George Eustice MP, environment Secretary, Jo Stevens MP, shadow culture secretary, Gordon Brown, former Prime Minister (2007-2010) and Professor Wendy Barclay, SAGE Adviser. The newspapers are reviewed by Vicki Young, BBC Deputy Political Editor and Camilla Tominey, Associate Editor, Daily Telegrap

programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow projects involving a listed Victorian lodge in Plaistow, London, an end terrace in Liverpool and a former pub in Blackburn

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks offer advice to two teams at the Highland Centre in Edinburgh, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp pays a visit to a camera obscura in the city

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is back on home turf in north Wales as he takes a journey exploring how his faith has shaped his life and his singing, catching up with composer Howard Goodall and the Rev Canon Mark Oakley along the way. Aled sings a couple of songs that are special to him and picks his favourite hymns for the programme, including Guide Me, O Thou Great Jehovah and How Great Thou Art

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades rescues three items about to be dumped at an Altrincham tip, with the experts transforming a table, a record player and a farmhouse chair

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a couple search for a country home close to the coast in Hampshire. While in the area, she also pays a visit the Mary Rose Museum

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway heads to the banks of the Menai Strait on a walk between the Britannia Bridge and the Menai Suspension Bridge. His guides are local Women's Institute members, which seems apt as it was on Anglesey that Britain's first ever WI meeting was held over a hundred years ago. Later, Derek treks between two captivating castles at Caerphilly and Castell Coch, and even gets a chance to dress up as a medieval knight

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Highlights of the night's fundraising activities, which were hosted by Kiri Pritchard-McLean, including a performance by singer-songwriter Kizzy Crawford

programme.title: Coal House
programme.desc: The three families reflect on their experiences of life as it was in the mining communities of the 1920s, talking about the bonds of friendship they have formed as well as the fun and hardship they have been through

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich assist a woman in Hampshire, whose front garden is out of control, with lots of messy shrubs and a front door the postman cannot find. They also help animal lover Sue in Lymington, who would like to share her garden with the hedgehogs that live in the neighbourhood

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison explores the powers of the great outdoors to provide a tonic in troubled times as she immerses herself in nature, from forest bathing to wild swimming. There are also films from the Countryfile archives about the health-giving prowess of the UK's green havens, including John Craven finding out how nature helped heal wartime wounds, and Anita Rani visiting a special farm giving respite to refugees

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes from Saturday's performances. The two couples with the lowest combined scores will take part in a dance-off before Shirley Ballas, Craig Revel Horwood and guest judge Anton Du Beke decide who will be the third pair to leave the contest. With music by the Vamps

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Lyra crosses into Will's world and finds it to be very different to her own, and after encountering the enigmatic Charles Latrom, the pair head to find a scholar to help get answers about Dust. Will is shocked to discover he has grandparents, but quickly realises he can't trust them, while a calculating Mrs Coulter pushes MacPhail to act after Graves' strong performance during the interrogation of a witch consul

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: The first episode of Steve McQueen's drama anthology based on the real-life experiences of London's West Indian community from the late-1960s to the mid-1980s. The film tells the story of the Old Bailey trial of Frank Crichlow, the owner of West Indian restaurant Mangrove in London's Notting Hill, who in 1970 was one of nine men and women wrongly arrested and charged with incitement to riot and affray after a protest about racial discrimination by the police. Starring Shaun Parkes, Letitia Wright and Malachi Kirby

programme.title: EastEnders

programme.desc: Jac rushes in a mystery patient, whose arrival sets in motion in a deepening medical crisis that could have serious repercussions for her secret relationship with Kian. Cameron basks in the glory of being declared a hero - prompting Nicky to issue him with an ultimatum

programme.desc: Three critically ill babies arrive within 24 hours. Elsewhere, a boy is brought in by an ambulance, and doctors struggle to work out why he is slipping in and out of consciousness
