programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi heads to Ramsbottom in Greater Manchester to lend her expertise to Charlotte Belcher, who has been running the 14-bedroom Grant Arms Hotel for two and a half years. Despite its attractive, central location, the 19th-century listed venue has developed a bad reputation as a hang-out for a 'rough crowd' - particularly at the bar. With no profits to pay for renovations, Charlotte has enlisted her husband and two children to help turn the Grant Arms around, but her work-life balance is starting to tip drastically away from home, and the beleaguered hotelier hopes Alex will find a way to boost the hotel's income while also restoring her private life

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the DIY SOS team transform the home of a police officer who was a victim of the Westminster Bridge terrorist attack in March 2017, suffering horrific injuries which left him wheelchair bound. Months after the attack took place, Kris still resides in hospital as his north London home is inaccessible for him, so the team and hundreds of volunteers work to get Kris living back at home to his family

programme.title: Ghost Nation

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Monkey Life

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: The vets examine 17-year-old chimpanzee Freddie, who is lacking his usual zest for life, and use a fibre-optic camera to look inside his intestines and identify the problem. programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the world of the pterosaur, a flying creature that occupied the skies 200 million years ago. Visiting sites around the world, from the Dorset coast to New Mexico, he explains how the reptiles evolved to defy the forces of gravity, and examines the features of their anatomy that can still be seen in present-day descendents. He also discovers why they became extinct after dominating the skies for millions of years

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Having overcome a bout of health problems, septuagenarians Ena and John Ryman are keen to live a little, and that means selling their Guildford home and searching for a property on the Jurassic Coast. With a budget of up to £500,000, Seetha Hallett shows them round an unusual upside down thatch in Lyme Regis and a restored Georgian apartment in Sidmouth

programme.title: Go Kart Go (1964)
programme.desc: A group of children builds a go kart for a race, but a rival team plots to sabotage their efforts. Family drama, starring Dennis Waterman and Frazer Hines

programme.title: The Face at the Window (1939)
programme.desc: In 1880, Paris is terrorised by a hideous murderer. An innocent man is accused of being the killer by a respected aristocrat who longs to get him out of the way so he can seduce his fiancee - but may also have other reasons for wanting a scapegoat. Horror, starring Tod Slaughter and John Warwick

programme.title: Turn the Key Softly (1953)
programme.desc: Three women are released from prison and set about the difficult task of staying on the straight and narrow. Drama, starring Joan Collins, Yvonne Mitchell and Kathleen Harrison

programme.title: The October Man (1947)
programme.desc: A chemist with suicidal tendencies is falsely accused of murder - and his precarious mental state is further upset by taunts from the real killer. Psychological thriller, adapted from a novel by Eric Ambler, starring John Mills, Joan Greenwood and Joyce Carey

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A couple with a budget of £80,000 fly out to Puerto Vallarta on Mexico's Pacific coast in search of a holiday home they can rent out to fund a trip round the world. The glorious weather and beaches are matched by bargain-priced properties, including a seaview apartment, a family home with separate studio and a coastal penthouse boasting its own pool

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams recounts the extraordinary tale of a Chicago-style turf-war gunfight on the Hampshire-Wiltshire border during the Second World War and Ennis Smith reveals how she broke the rules to marry her soldier boyfriend Clarence

programme.title: Capital Conversation
programme.desc: Author and entrepreneur Michael Hayman interviews the business leaders at the heart of London's most exciting success stories

programme.title: The Ronnie Wood Show

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: A journey along the Thames from the Cotswold Hills to the North Sea, concentrating on the lives of the water voles, swans and eels that call it home, and highlighting the escalating battle to control the waterways despite technological advances in flood management. As London continues to sink faster than Venice, the untamed nature of the river is revealed

programme.title: Maxwell
programme.desc: One-off drama reconstructing the final stages in the life of Czech-born media mogul Robert Maxwell. Under intense pressure, with cracks showing in his business empire, his marriage in trouble, health concerns and a massive pension scheme fraud to hide, he escapes to the privacy of his yacht near the Canary Islands. David Suchet and Patricia Hodge star

programme.title: London Town (2016)
programme.desc: A 14-year-old boy living in 1970s London has his life changed forever when his is introduced to The Clash by his estranged mother. Drama, starring Tom Hughes, Jonathan Rhys Meyers and Dougray Scott

programme.title: Angelica (2017)
programme.desc: A couple in Victorian London endure an unusual series of supernatural happenings following the birth of their child. Drama, starring Jena Malone, Janet McTeer and Ed Stoppard

programme.title: Run
programme.desc: A Polish cleaner living in London discovers her gambling addict boyfriend has been killed in a vicious attack. She then unearths his secret relationship with another woman, and finds herself in grave danger. The last of four dramas telling the stories of seemingly unconnected people facing life-changing decisions, starring Katharina Schuttler, Jaime Winstone and Marcin Dorocinski

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours narrated by Dewyne Lindsay, this time exploring Canary Wharf and Southbank.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner

programme.title: Live Grand Slam of Darts
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening session from day one, featuring eight group matches at Ricoh Arena in Coventry. The tournament was won last year by Gerwyn Price who defeated Peter Wright 16-6 in the final. That win meant that Price defended the title he won back in 2018, after he beat Gary Anderson 16-13 in a highly controversial final. The tournament will be held behind closed doors due to the ongoing coronavirus pandemic.

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Action from day three of the First Test at Trent Bridge, as the match passed the halfway point. The sides last met here in the Test arena in 2005, and only three players are still active for their countries from that match, with England's Kevin Pietersen and Ian Bell, and Australian captain Michael Clarke still involved. The tourists have been heavily reliant on Clarke in the recent years as they struggle to adapt with a relatively inexperienced outfit, while Pietersen and Bell have remained mainstays of the English middle order

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Action from day four of the First Test at Trent Bridge, where the second innings was likely to be taking shape ahead of the final day. When England last hosted Australia on this ground in this format in 2005 they claimed a crucial three-wicket victory that gave them a 2-1 lead in the series heading into the final Test at the Oval, a lead that they did not relinquish as they triumphed in an Ashes series for the first time since 1987

programme.title: The Ashes - Verdict
programme.desc: A panel of experts offer their analysis of the fourth day of the First Test between England and Australia at Trent Bridge, as the sides continued the five-match series

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Action from the fifth and final day of the Third Test at Old Trafford, where the five-match series moved a step closer to completion. The sides last faced each other here in the Test arena in 2005 in a thrilling encounter that saw Australia defy the odds on the final day to secure a draw, with Brett Lee and Glenn McGrath surviving the final 24 balls after Ricky Ponting's man-of-the-match-winning innings of 156 had kept the England attack at bay

programme.title: The Ashes - Verdict
programme.desc: A panel of experts offer their analysis of the fifth day of the First Test between England and Australia at Trent Bridge, as the opening contest in the five-match series concluded

programme.title: The Ashes
programme.desc: England v Australia. Action from day five of the First Test at Trent Bridge, where the opening contest of the five-match series was brought to a conclusion. Having decided to replace their head coach Mickey Arthur with former batsman Darren Lehmann just 16 days prior to the commencement of this series, Australia will have hoped to claim an unlikely victory in this encounter that could spur them on in the coming Test matches

programme.title: EFL Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Liverpool v Cardiff City. Action from the showpiece match at Wembley Stadium, where the winner of the first major trophy of the 2011/12 season was decided. The Reds had defeated the likes of Stoke City, Chelsea and Manchester City to reach the final and went into the match as clear favourites in their first visit to the national stadium since it was rebuilt. However, the Bluebirds, who defeated Crystal Palace on penalties in the semi-finals, had been in good form in the Championship and will have fancied their chances of producing an upset

programme.title: Live Grand Slam of Darts
programme.desc: Coverage of the opening session from day one, featuring eight group matches at Ricoh Arena in Coventry. The tournament was won last year by Gerwyn Price who defeated Peter Wright 16-6 in the final. That win meant that Price defended the title he won back in 2018, after he beat Gary Anderson 16-13 in a highly controversial final. The tournament will be held behind closed doors due to the ongoing coronavirus pandemic.
